task_completion.description: Verify the observation location is set to ALMA in Chile with the correct coordinates and altitude.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Stellarium's 'Location' window (F6) is open, showing latitude around S 23° 1' (or -23.02°), longitude around W 67° 45' (or -67.75°), and altitude 5058m, or the location is explicitly set to ALMA.

task_completion.description: Verify that both atmosphere and landscape rendering are disabled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The landscape (ground) and atmosphere (sky glow) are not visible in the Stellarium view. The background behind Saturn should be a pure black starfield, and the atmosphere/landscape toggle buttons on the bottom toolbar should be unhighlighted/off.

task_completion.description: Verify that Saturn is targeted and viewed through a high-power telescopic zoom.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Saturn is selected, centered, and zoomed in significantly so that the planet and its rings take up a large portion of the viewing area.

task_completion.description: Verify the observation dates and times are set correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Stellarium's 'Date and time' window (F5) or the bottom toolbar shows the date being set to 2017-10-15, 2021-10-15, or 2025-03-23, with the time set to 06:00.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the required article text file with the correct content.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (like gedit, nano) or terminal output shows the file '/home/ga/Desktop/saturn_rings_article.txt' containing the title 'The Disappearing Rings', the word 'Saturn', the years 2017, 2021, and 2025, and an explanation of the 'edge-on' appearance.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent interacted with the Stellarium GUI to configure the view and take screenshots.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Stellarium application window is visible in the screenshots, showing genuine interaction with its menus and toolbars, rather than the agent just modifying configuration files blindly via terminal.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not download images of Saturn from the internet.
integrity.visual_evidence: No web browser is open searching for 'Saturn ring plane crossing' or downloading external images to use as the screenshots.

integrity.description: Ensure the article text was genuinely created by the agent.
integrity.visual_evidence: The article text is typed or constructed in a text editor or via terminal commands (like echo/cat), not copy-pasted from a pre-existing external file or web page.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified facts for the VLM verifier: The exact coordinates for ALMA are latitude -23.02°, longitude -67.75°, and altitude 5058m. The target dates are exactly 2017-10-15, 2021-10-15, and 2025-03-23 at 06:00 UTC. The task requires disabling both atmosphere and landscape rendering in Stellarium. The article must be saved to /home/ga/Desktop/saturn_rings_article.txt and contain the title 'The Disappearing Rings', target 'Saturn', the three observation years, and an explanation of the 'edge-on' appearance. At least 3 screenshots must be captured and saved to /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium/.
